title: Mini Project
overview: Congratulations! You've learned all the fundamentals of Python. Now it's time to apply everything by building a complete project of your choice.

blocks.title: Choose Your Project
blocks.body: You've learned variables, conditionals, loops, functions, lists, dictionaries, strings, and file handling. Now pick one of four projects to build from scratch.
bullets: **Student Management System** — Add, view, update, and delete student records
**Expense Tracker** — Log expenses, view totals, and filter by category
**Quiz Application** — Create quizzes with multiple choice questions and scoring
**To-Do List** — Add tasks, mark complete, and save/load from a file

blocks.title: This is Your Graduation Project
blocks.text: Just like a chef who learns all the techniques before cooking their signature dish, you've learned all the Python ingredients. Now put them together to create something amazing!

blocks.title: Minimum Requirements
blocks.body: Your project must include the following features to be complete.
bullets: At least one loop (for or while)
Use a list or dictionary to store data
**Bonus**: File handling (save/load data from a file)

blocks.content: Start by planning your project on paper: What features will it have? What data structure will you use? How will functions be organized? Write the pseudocode first, then translate to Python.

blocks.title: Project Template — Student Management System
blocks.expectedOutput:
1. Add Student
2. View All
3. Exit
Choose:

blocks.question: Which project would you like to build and why? Think about which one you'd find most useful in your daily life.
blocks.answer: Any project is great for practice! The Student Management System teaches CRUD operations. The Expense Tracker teaches data analysis. The Quiz App teaches logic and scoring. The To-Do List teaches file persistence. Choose what excites you!

blocks.title: Project Menu
blocks.instructions: Write a menu program showing 4 project options. Let the user pick one (1-4) and print a confirmation.
blocks.starterCode:
print("1. Student Management System")
print("2. Expense Tracker")
print("3. Quiz Application")
print("4. To-Do List")
choice = int(input("Choose (1-4): "))
projects = ["", "Student Management System", "Expense Tracker", "Quiz Application", "To-Do List"]
blocks.solution:
print("1. Student Management System")
print("2. Expense Tracker")
print("3. Quiz Application")
print("4. To-Do List")
choice = int(input("Choose (1-4): "))
projects = ["", "Student Management System", "Expense Tracker", "Quiz Application", "To-Do List"]
print(f"You selected: {projects[choice]}")
blocks.hint: Use a list to map choice numbers to project names.

keyTakeaways.text: You've learned all Python fundamentals — now apply them!
Use functions to organize code into clear sections
Bonus: add file saving/loading for data persistence
Build something YOU want to use — that's the best motivation

whatYouLearned: How to plan and structure a complete Python project
How to use functions, loops, conditionals, and data structures together
How to handle file saving/loading for persistence
How to build a menu-driven interactive program
How to apply everything you've learned in one complete project
